title: Add cart merge journal persistence
verify: Cart Merge Module resolves through the real Medusa container and persists to local PostgreSQL.
The migration adds only the custom journal model and indexes and does not alter Medusa core cart tables.
A second independent plan for the same source_cart_id is rejected by a durable unique constraint.
A journal record can be read after a fresh runtime/module resolution.

verify.summary: Manual T2 verification passed. The required packet was ready and hash-matched; strict doctor passed; the Medusa migration was up-to-date; two independent Medusa exec processes proved PostgreSQL persistence and durable source-cart uniqueness; direct migration and database inspection proved only the custom cart_merge table and indexes were added; backend typecheck and Memory Bank lint passed.

evidence_required: Backend typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
constraints: Use a supported Medusa custom module and migration.
Store core cart/customer references as scalar IDs without cross-module database foreign keys.
Do not modify or duplicate Medusa Cart or LineItem persistence.
invariants: The immutable merge plan is keyed by Medusa Product Variant ID.
No credentials or customer contact data enter the journal.
purpose: Create the durable exactly-once and recovery boundary required before authenticated cart merge.
success_outcome: A Medusa-managed custom module persists one immutable merge journal per source cart and survives runtime restart.
anti_goals: Do not create parallel cart or line-item tables.
Do not implement the merge API or storefront UI in this task.
Do not run destructive production migrations.

forbidden_scope: Medusa core source or core cart table changes
stop_conditions: The supported Medusa module model cannot express the required unique source constraint.
Implementation requires changing Medusa core cart persistence.
PostgreSQL read/write cannot be exercised through the module boundary.
